mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals resilient household spending and near-term revenue growth for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 705692.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, restrictive policy raises equity discount rates and pressures valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.33 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% have higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations with t-test p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deterioration in labor demand and rising recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 3.7 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum remains high while FEDFUNDS is already restrictive, markets price a longer high-rate path and lower equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 308.85 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPIAUCSL and high-FEDFUNDS trigger has a positive mean 20-day S&P 500 return or if its downside hit rate is below 55% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST during a high FEDFUNDS regime signals that rate-sensitive demand is absorbing tight financial conditions, improving cyclical growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1560.0 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger is followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if positive-return frequency is below 55% in walk-forward out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month INDPRO growth signals improving production volumes and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 102.6651 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO observations have lower mean 5-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with elevated CPIAUCSL inflation momentum signals overheating rather than benign expansion, increasing expected policy restraint and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 22490.692 and CPIAUCSL was 308.85
failure_condition: Reject if overheating-trigger observations have positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if their mean return is not at least 100 basis points below unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth with UNRATE below 4.0% while FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% signals a tight labor market that delays policy easing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS 3-month average gain > 150000 while FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 157232.0, UNRATE was 3.7, and FEDFUNDS was 5.33 in the latest available snapshot
regime_hint: restrictive policy with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tight-labor and high-FEDFUNDS trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if average triggered return exceeds baseline.
